title: Leptostigma setulosum
intro: Leptostigma setulosum, also known as Indian spindle tree, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its small, white flowers and woody stem. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Leptostigma setulosum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Leptostigma setulosum is commonly prepared as a decoction, powder, or infusion, often used to make herbal teas, tonics, and capsules.
